top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 116, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
7,546ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
65ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic 66 Degrés Sud – La Cyclo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of 66 Degrés Sud – La Cyclo. Train it until it feels boring.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 116 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 7,546ft of climbing on mountain passes does to your power file on race day.
At 66 Degrés Sud – La Cyclo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
116 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 116 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
66 Degrés Sud – La Cyclo's mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 116 miles.
Tempo is 66 Degrés Sud – La Cyclo's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 116 miles on mountain passes. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for 66 Degrés Sud – La Cyclo’s 116 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at 66 Degrés Sud – La Cyclo. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, VO2max, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 116 miles of mountain passes in Canet-en-Roussillon, France.
